evidence: As we integrate artificial intelligence technologies into our processes, these technologies may present business, compliance and reputational risks.
Our integration of these technologies, whether developed internally or procured through our third-party service providers, into our processes may result in new or expanded risks.
advanced artificial intelligence attacks, which can compromise our ability to operate, and the confidentiality, availability, and integrity of data in our systems
Such risks and liabilities include enhanced governmental or regulatory scrutiny, litigation, compliance issues, ethical concerns, confidentiality or security risks
The use of AI can lead to unintended consequences, including generating content that is inaccurate, misleading or otherwise flawed, or that results in unintended biases and discriminatory outcomes

limitations: ai_investment_intensity not scorable: excerpts contain no disclosure of AI-specific capex, named infrastructure, vendors, or budget amounts; only risk-factor language referencing AI integration is present.

ai_narrative_concreteness scored conservatively at 3: the excerpts reference AI integration into processes but provide no deployment status, named initiatives, or quantified outcomes; language is risk-disclosure framing rather than operational detail.

ai_risk_disclosure_depth scored 4: risk-factor section is present and describes company-specific mechanisms (regulatory scrutiny, IP infringement, inaccurate outputs harming reputation/financials, cybersecurity attack vectors) but no quantification or named governance/oversight bodies are disclosed in these excerpts.
